questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the coastal village of Dandi located?
questions.explanation: Dandi is a coastal village in the Indian state of Gujarat.

questions.q: What did Gandhi famously make at Dandi to defy British law?
questions.explanation: At Dandi, Gandhi made salt from seawater in defiance of the salt tax.

questions.q: Who led the famous 1930 march that ended at Dandi?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the 1930 march to the coastal village of Dandi.

questions.q: In which year did Gandhi reach Dandi and break the salt law?
questions.explanation: Gandhi reached Dandi and broke the salt law in 1930.

questions.q: What natural substance did Gandhi pick up from the shore at Dandi?
questions.explanation: Gandhi picked up a handful of natural salt at Dandi to break the salt law.

questions.q: The act of making salt at Dandi launched which larger nationwide campaign?
questions.explanation: Breaking the salt law at Dandi launched the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.q: From which ashram did Gandhi's march to Dandi begin?
questions.explanation: The march to Dandi set out from Gandhi's Sabarmati Ashram near Ahmedabad.

questions.q: Dandi sits on the coast of which body of water?
questions.explanation: Dandi lies on the western coast of India along the Arabian Sea.

questions.q: What kind of resistance did Gandhi's salt-making at Dandi represent?
questions.explanation: The act at Dandi became a landmark of nonviolent resistance against colonial rule.

questions.q: Dandi became famous as the destination of which event?
questions.explanation: Dandi was the destination of Gandhi's 1930 Salt March.

questions.q: Approximately how long was the march from Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi?
questions.explanation: The march from Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi covered roughly 240 miles (about 385 km).

questions.q: On which date did Gandhi break the salt law at Dandi?
questions.explanation: Gandhi broke the salt law at Dandi on the morning of 6 April 1930.

questions.q: On which date did the march to Dandi begin from Sabarmati Ashram?
questions.explanation: The march to Dandi began on 12 March 1930 from Sabarmati Ashram.

questions.q: Roughly how many days did the march to Dandi take to complete?
questions.explanation: The march to Dandi took about 24 days, from 12 March to 6 April 1930.

questions.q: How many volunteers initially set out with Gandhi from Sabarmati on the march to Dandi?
questions.explanation: Gandhi set out for Dandi with about 78 chosen ashram volunteers, with crowds swelling along the route.

questions.q: The salt-tax defiance at Dandi was a violation of which colonial-era legislation?
options: The salt laws and salt monopoly
questions.explanation: Making salt at Dandi violated the British salt laws that monopolized and taxed salt production.

questions.q: Which district of Gujarat is the village of Dandi part of?
questions.explanation: Dandi falls within the Navsari district of Gujarat.

questions.q: Why did Gandhi choose to defy the salt law specifically, making Dandi the target?
options: Salt was a daily necessity taxed by the British
Salt was only used by the wealthy
questions.explanation: Salt was a daily necessity for all Indians, so taxing it made the Dandi protest universally resonant.

questions.q: What did Gandhi reportedly say as he raised the salt at Dandi, signalling defiance?
options: He declared the end of the war
He declared he was shaking the foundations of the British Empire
He called for a general strike
questions.explanation: Gandhi is widely reported to have declared at Dandi that he was shaking the foundations of the British Empire.

questions.q: After the act at Dandi, salt-law defiance spread to many coastal sites; one major salt-raid site nearby was:
questions.explanation: Following Dandi, nonviolent salt raids spread, most famously to the Dharasana Salt Works in Gujarat.

questions.q: Which national memorial commemorating the Salt March was inaugurated at Dandi in 2019?
questions.explanation: The National Salt Satyagraha Memorial at Dandi was inaugurated in 2019 to commemorate the march.

questions.q: The Salt March to Dandi drew worldwide attention largely because of what?
options: International press coverage of the nonviolent protest
questions.explanation: Extensive international press coverage of the nonviolent march made Dandi a global symbol of resistance.

questions.q: Through which town did Gandhi's march to Dandi NOT pass on its route?
questions.explanation: The march route ran through Gujarat towns like Aslali, Nadiad and Surat; Pune is in Maharashtra and was not on the route.

questions.q: Gandhi sent a letter announcing his intention to break the salt law to which Viceroy before the march?
questions.explanation: Before the march to Dandi, Gandhi wrote to Viceroy Lord Irwin announcing his plan to defy the salt law.

questions.q: On which river's bank near Ahmedabad did the march to Dandi originate, at Sabarmati Ashram?
questions.explanation: Sabarmati Ashram, the march's starting point, stood on the bank of the Sabarmati river near Ahmedabad.

questions.q: Gandhi vowed not to return to Sabarmati Ashram until which goal was achieved, a pledge tied to the Dandi campaign?
options: Until he was elected to office
Until salt taxes were abolished and swaraj attained
questions.explanation: Gandhi pledged not to return to Sabarmati Ashram until India had won swaraj, a vow linked to the Dandi salt campaign.

questions.q: What was the approximate daily distance Gandhi and the marchers walked on the way to Dandi?
questions.explanation: The marchers covered roughly 10 to 12 miles per day on foot during the journey to Dandi.

questions.q: At which time of day did Gandhi famously break the salt law at the Dandi shore?
questions.explanation: Gandhi broke the salt law at the Dandi shore in the early morning of 6 April 1930.

questions.q: Which poet-leader and Congress figure was among those who joined Gandhi during the salt campaign around Dandi and Dharasana?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu joined the salt campaign and led the Dharasana raid following Gandhi's act at Dandi.

questions.q: The Dandi March is also commonly known by which alternative name?
questions.explanation: The march to Dandi is also known as the Salt Satyagraha or the Dandi Salt March.
